commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ae2fab76a80b81a5ca53d78d326a7f31545863e5)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an optional `identity` field to the CLI channel outbound runtime and unit tests that assert direct runtime text/media calls pass it to a mocked channel adapter.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +48. Total +51 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: cron delivery resolves and passes identity into outbound delivery, but Slack's registered text/media outbound methods send without identity. I did not run a live Slack reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a contributor or maintainer to repair the actual Slack outbound path and supply real behavior proof; this is not ready for automated merge cleanup.

target.title: fix(cli): forward per-agent identity through channel outbound send runtime (fixes #84297)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Per-agent identity overlay (`agents.list[<id>].identity`) is dropped when outbound messages go through the CLI send runtime (`deps.<channel>.sendMessage`), causing cron announce and heartbeat pushes to render under the app-level bot identity instead of the per-agent persona.
- **Root Cause**: `src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.ts` `buildContext()` constructs the channel-adapter context without `identity` — the field is absent from both `RuntimeSendOpts` and the context object, unlike the `deliverOutboundPayloadsInternal` path which correctly passes `identity` to channel adapters.
- **Fix**: Add `identity?: OutboundIdentity` to `RuntimeSendOpts` and forward `opts.identity` through `buildContext()` so the channel adapter receives it on every send branch (`sendText`, `sendMedia`, `sendPayload`).
- **What changed**:
  - `src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.ts` — added `OutboundIdentity` import, `identity` to `RuntimeSendOpts`, and `identity: opts.identity` in `buildContext()`
  - `src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts` — added two tests covering identity passthrough for `sendText` and `sendMedia`
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - The `deliverOutboundPayloadsInternal` path already passes `identity` correctly; no changes needed there
  - Slack adapter identity rendering logic is already correct — it just never received the field on this path
  - No config, migration, or plugin changes

### Reproduction

1. Configure a per-agent identity in `openclaw.json`:
   ```jsonc
   "agents": { "list": [{
     "id": "pulse",
     "identity": { "name": "Pulse", "emoji": "📟" },
     "heartbeat": { "every": "2h", "target": "slack", "to": "channel:C0XXX", "accountId": "default" }
   }]}
   ```
2. Verify the Slack app has `chat:write.customize` scope
3. Trigger a cron announce or heartbeat that sends to Slack via `deps.slack.sendMessage`
4. **Before this PR**: The message renders under the generic Slack app bot name and default icon
5. **After this PR**: The message renders under the per-agent persona ("Pulse" + 📟)

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#84297)**: `channel-outbound-send.ts` drops per-agent `identity` before reaching the Slack adapter, so `chat.postMessage` calls never receive `username`/`icon_emoji`/`icon_url` overrides on the CLI send path even though the reply path (fix in #38235) and the `deliverOutboundPayloadsInternal` path both include identity.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — test suite against `createChannelOutboundRuntimeSend` with a test-double channel adapter that asserts the received context fields

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1

 ✓ src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts (10 tests) 5ms
   ✓ createChannelOutboundRuntimeSend (10)
     ✓ routes media sends through sendMedia and preserves media access 1ms
     ✓ falls back to sendText for text-only sends 0ms
     ✓ preserves rendered html formatting through lazy text sends 0ms
     ✓ routes block sends through payload delivery 0ms
     ✓ accepts plugin outbound thread and reply aliases 0ms
     ✓ forwards Slack threadTs alias to threadId 0ms
     ✓ prefers canonical thread fields over Slack aliases 0ms
     ✓ passes identity through to sendText when opts.identity is set 0ms
     ✓ passes identity through to sendMedia when opts.identity is set 0ms
     ✓ falls back to sendText when media is present but sendMedia is unavailable 0ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
   Start at  20:29:18
   Duration  2.31s

```

**Observed result after fix**: The two new identity passthrough tests confirm that when `opts.identity` is set to `{ name: "Pulse", emoji: "📟" }`, the channel adapter receives it on both `sendText` and `sendMedia` branches. The eight pre-existing tests continue to pass — identity forwarding is additive and does not alter any existing behavior when `opts.identity` is omitted.

**What was not tested**: Live Slack round-trip was not driven (requires `chat:write.customize` scope and a provisioned Slack app). The `sendPayload` branch with blocks also forwards `identity` via the spread pattern but is not covered by a dedicated test case in this PR.

**Repro confirmation**: The new test cases pass on the fix branch (identity reaches the channel adapter) and would fail on upstream/main because `buildContext()` does not include `identity` in the returned context object.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: Callers not yet passing `identity` in `RuntimeSendOpts` see no behavioral change — the field is optional and defaults to `undefined`, preserving existing behavior.
  **Mitigation**: The field is `OutboundIdentity | undefined`; old callers continue to work identically.
- **Risk**: Channel adapters that are identity-aware (Slack, Telegram) will now receive the field when the caller passes it. Non-identity-aware adapters ignore unknown fields.
  **Mitigation**: The field was always part of the channel adapter context signature (`identity?: OutboundIdentity` in `outbound.types.ts`); this change only fixes the plumbing that was missing it.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI
- [x] Core (outbound send runtime)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #84297
